title: Attention & Transformer (Q,K,V) + Graphs
focus: Self-attention end-to-end; multi-head, RoPE, FlashAttention, MQA/GQA.

items.label: Max Area OF Island
interviewQuestions: Is this BFS, DFS, or Union-Find? Defend the choice over the other two.
Walk through complexity in terms of V and E. Where do those costs come from?
How would you handle disconnected components, self-loops, or duplicate edges?

items.label: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
interviewQuestions: Is this BFS, DFS, or Union-Find? Defend the choice over the other two.
Walk through complexity in terms of V and E. Where do those costs come from?
How would you handle disconnected components, self-loops, or duplicate edges?

items.label: Self-attention(Q, K, V) end-to-end
interviewQuestions: Walk me through self-attention(Q, K, V) end-to-end.
What does multi-head attention buy you over a single head?

items.label: Encoder-decoder, positional encoding, residuals
interviewQuestions: Walk me through the transformer block: attention → add+norm → FFN → add+norm.
Compare absolute vs relative vs RoPE positional encodings.

items.label: Sparse, linear, FlashAttention, MQA, GQA
interviewQuestions: What problem does FlashAttention solve, and how?
Compare MHA, MQA, and GQA — KV-cache trade-offs.

items.label: Code self-attention, multi-head attention, and a tiny transformer
interviewQuestions: Implement scaled dot-product self-attention and track the shapes of Q, K, V, scores, and output.
How do masks change self-attention for causal language modeling?
What changes when you split attention into multiple heads and then concatenate them?
